summary: Linked several already-complete objects by updating build object status, compiler flags, GALE01 symbols/splits, and two small C source details. The automated decomp.dev report showed +2428 linked code bytes, +336 matched data bytes, +1288 linked data bytes, and 5 new matches, mostly for sysdolphin/baselib/psappsrt extab/extabindex/bss/sbss plus bytecode .data.
what_changed: Promoted Object entries from NonMatching to Matching for MSL/math_data.c, sysdolphin/baselib/psappsrt.c, sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B27.c, and sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B2B.c.

Added extra_cflags=["-Cpp_exceptions on"] to sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c, psdisp.c, and psappsrt.c object declarations; particle.c and psdisp.c remained NonMatching, while psappsrt.c became Matching.
Changed psappsrt.c storage declarations so HSD_PSAppSrt_804D7958 is a static u16[4] definition and HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 is a global HSD_ObjAllocData definition instead of extern declarations.

Adjusted GALE01 symbols for psappsrt-related data: extab/extabindex entries became numeric local labels, HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 size changed from 0x30 to 0x2C, and HSD_PSAppSrt_804DEA68/804DEA6C became local @151/@152 sdata2 symbols.
Split the monolithic HSD_ByteCode_804074F0 data symbol into several lbl_804074F0-style data:string symbols.
Moved the .sdata2 range 0x804DEB10-0x804DEC00 in splits.txt from sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_40FF.c to sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34.c.
Changed mnDiagram_ConvertDistanceForDisplay to divide by unsuffixed double literal 30.4788 instead of float literal 30.4788F.
smart_moves: Used build metadata and symbol ownership fixes to link completed objects without unnecessary source churn.
Enabled -Cpp_exceptions on the psappsrt/particle/psdisp area, which aligned with the bot-reported psappsrt extab and extabindex matches.
Corrected C storage class and definition-vs-extern status for psappsrt globals so the compiler emits the expected bss/sbss objects.
Treated padding and symbol size precisely: HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 was recorded as 0x2C rather than absorbing the full 0x30 gap before the next bss symbol.
Broke bytecode .data into individual string symbols, which the bot report indicates completed the bytecode .data match.
decomp_lessons: Object matching can be blocked by configure.py status, compiler flags, symbols.txt, or splits.txt even when the C body is effectively done.
For CodeWarrior-style baselib objects with exception metadata, -Cpp_exceptions on can be necessary for extab/extabindex section matching.
Use actual definitions, not extern declarations, for globals/static data that must be emitted by the object currently being matched.
Static scope in C should be reflected as local symbols in symbols.txt where appropriate; exporting local constants can prevent data matching.
Do not let symbol sizes include alignment padding unless the underlying object really owns those bytes.
In f64 arithmetic, a float-suffixed literal can change codegen/data versus an unsuffixed double literal.
naming_conventions: Compiler/local exception table entries may use numeric local labels such as @141, @142, @153, and @154 instead of address-derived @etb_... or @eti_... names.
Local sdata2 constants can be represented as numeric @ labels with scope:local, as with @151 and @152 replacing HSD_PSAppSrt_804DEA68 and HSD_PSAppSrt_804DEA6C.
Unknown address-based string data can be represented as lbl_8040.... data:string symbols rather than a single semantic global object name.
HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 remains a global HSD-style object name, while HSD_PSAppSrt_804D7958 is static in the C source despite its HSD-style name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: After symbol/split/flag corrections, switch completed objects from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Use -Cpp_exceptions on when matching objects whose extab/extabindex output must be linked.
Fix section ownership in config/GALE01/splits.txt before promoting objects that depend on that section range.
Split large anonymous data blobs into smaller typed string symbols when evidence shows the data is a sequence of strings.
For f64 casts/divisions, test whether constants need to be unsuffixed doubles instead of float-suffixed literals.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the PR slice.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 f3b89a0 -> 8563b26: linked code 38.81% (+0.06%, +2428 bytes), matched data 38.80% (+0.03%, +336 bytes), linked data 33.14% (+0.11%, +1288 bytes).
Automated report listed 5 new matches: psappsrt extabindex +72, psappsrt .bss +48, psappsrt extab +48, psappsrt .sbss +8, and bytecode .data +2.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining NonMatching baselib objects that may need -Cpp_exceptions on for extab/extabindex output.
Audit HSD_ObjAllocData symbols for sizes that incorrectly include alignment padding.
Search for monolithic bytecode or string data symbols that could be split into lbl_8040.... data:string entries.
Search for f64 calculations using float-suffixed literals that may need unsuffixed double constants.
Verify nearby sysdolphin/baselib .sdata2 ranges in splits.txt are assigned to the object that actually owns them.
